canonical_definition: A dataset containing 10,000 human-labeled preference pairs on the helpfulness and harmlessness of responses, used for fine-tuning and aligning large language models for safety.
papers: A Distributional Approach to Uncertainty-Aware Preference Alignment Using Offline Demonstrations
GenARM: Reward Guided Generation with Autoregressive Reward Model for Test-Time Alignment

relationships.paper: A Distributional Approach to Uncertainty-Aware Preference Alignment Using Offline Demonstrations
relationships.evidence: the PKU-SafeRLHF-10K dataset (Ji et al., 2023; Dai et al., 2024), which contains human-labeled preference data on the helpfulness and harmlessness of prompt-response pairs (Section 6.4)
